spec_match.rationale: Six common clip shapes shown side-by-side sharp-vs-rounded is a clear visualisation of the spec syntax, but it's a single fixed radius per shape and never demonstrates per-vertex clamping or varying radii.

interactive.rationale: Live-probe panel and CSS-only renders, but the only interaction is the live-probe CSS.supports readout. No radius slider, no shape picker — closer to a code-card-with-live-render than a hands-on tool.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Lede promises six clip shapes shown sharp-vs-rounded; that's what the page delivers.

edge_cases.rationale: No demo of acute/obtuse vertex differences, no zero-radius case, no over-clamping case (radius > segment/2), no concave-corner test, no fill-rule interaction. Happy path only.

design_system.rationale: All chrome from CSS variables, support banner uses --accent-emerald / --accent-rose for ok/bad, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add a radius slider that drives the whole gallery
openQuestions.detail: A single radius slider that drives all six cards turns the gallery into an instrument and immediately surfaces the per-vertex clamping difference between shapes with acute vs obtuse vertices.

openQuestions.title: Add a 'sharp == round 0' sanity card
openQuestions.detail: A seventh card overlaying polygon(round 0px, ...) at 50% with polygon(...) proves round 0 is identity and would expose rendering bugs that don't treat them as equivalent.

openQuestions.title: Show acute-vertex clamping
openQuestions.detail: Pick a star or arrow with one ~30° vertex and crank radius beyond tan(15°)*segment/2. The spec clamps; show clamp vs naive interpretation with a tooltip explaining why.

openQuestions.title: Include shape-outside and offset-path
openQuestions.detail: polygon(round) also applies to shape-outside and offset-path. A gallery is the right place to show all three contexts in one grid.

summary: Nice visual reference but barely interactive — six fixed renders next to a live probe. A single radius slider would lift it from gallery to instrument and unlock the per-vertex clamping story.
